Only pitcher to hit 3 HR in a postseason game.

THE NUMBERS THEY DON'T KEEP IN THE RECORD BOOKS
⟡ CBT Dollars Saved — Deferred Contract

In 2024, Shohei Ohtani signed an unprecedented contract with the LA Dodgers in which he deferred 97% of his salary for 10 years to save the LA Dodgers money on the Competitive Balance Tax. This is how much he’s saved the Dodgers so far:

$54,426,477
LIVE · Accruing in real time
$26.31M saved per season at the 110% CBT marginal rate via the 4.43% present-value discount on $680M of deferred salary.
2024: $26.33M · 2025: $26.31M · 2026: pro-rated daily
📹 Confirmed On-Camera F-Bombs

Ohtani famously curses in English as it is seen as less severe than cursing in his native Japanese. Not that American audiences seem to mind.

4 F-BOMBS
Ohtani has been caught using an F-Bomb on camera four times, most recently in April 2026 after getting hit in the elbow on a pick-off attempt.

Quest Progress
OHTANI'S JOURNEY TRACKER
Goals Written at Age 16  ·  Progress at Age 31

At 16, Shohei Ohtani already knew what he wanted to be: one of the greatest MLB players of all time. He broke this goal down into a set of smaller goals, roadmapped to each year of his career. Though this list of goals was likely put together when Ohtani assumed he would only be pitching, the more general goals have not only been accomplished, but on or ahead of schedule. This page tracks Ohtani’s progress toward the goals he set as a 16-year-old growing up in Japan.

Accomplished
Partial
